summary: This is a brief teaser post directing readers to Joel's Inc. magazine column about the value of middle managers. The post includes a quote about employee unhappiness and complaints affecting productivity, suggesting the column explores how Joel came to appreciate the role middle managers play in organizations. The framing implies Joel initially held the common programmer-culture skepticism toward middle management before changing his view.
key_points: Employee unhappiness can become self-reinforcing, with complaining replacing productive work
Joel's perspective on middle managers evolved from skepticism to appreciation
The post serves as a teaser for his Inc. magazine column on organizational management
money_quotes: I thought you should know that people are really unhappy, and it's starting to make it so that people just complain all day, instead of doing their work, and that's not good.
key_insight: Middle managers serve a vital function in organizations by addressing employee unhappiness before it spirals into a culture of complaint that undermines productivity.
